```{r classifier-6w, cache=TRUE}
    interpretation <- "Limited predictive performance - microbiome signal for malaria history is weak"
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("\nInterpretation: %s\n", interpretation))
  
  # Taxonomic level analysis
  cat(sprintf("\nTaxonomic Level Performance Summary:\n"))
  for(tax_level in taxonomic_levels) {
    level_results <- successful_results[successful_results$Taxonomic_Level == tax_level, ]
    if(nrow(level_results) > 0) {
      avg_performance <- mean(level_results$Balanced_Accuracy)
      best_performance <- max(level_results$Balanced_Accuracy)
      cat(sprintf("- %s: Avg=%.3f, Best=%.3f (%d successful models)\n", 
                  tax_level, avg_performance, best_performance, nrow(level_results)))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("- %s: No successful models\n", tax_level))
    }
  }
}

cat("\nGenerated Files:\n")
cat("=== SixWeek Folder ===\n")
cat("- SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_results.csv: Detailed performance metrics\n")
cat("- SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_performance.pdf: Performance comparison plot\n")
cat("- SixWeekmicrobiome_performance_heatmap.pdf: Summary heatmap\n")
cat("\n=== Root Folder ===\n")
cat("- SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData: Complete R workspace\n")

```


## Six-Week Model: Extraction and Visualization (Figures 7A-B, S3)

Verbatim from `SixWeekModelExtractionAndVisualization.R`. Objects
`all_results`, `results_summary`, `successful_results`, `meco_dataset`,
and `taxonomic_levels` carry over from the previous chunk.

```{r classifier-6w-extract, cache=TRUE, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
library(gridExtra)
library(viridis)
library(pheatmap)
`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (is.null(a)) b else a

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 1. Load Results and Identify Best Model
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("=== LOADING RESULTS ===\n")

# Check if results are already loaded
if(!exists("all_results") || !exists("results_summary")) {
  if(file.exists("SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")) {
    load("SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")
    cat("[OK] Loaded results from RData file\n")
  } else if(file.exists("microbiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")) {
    load("microbiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")
    cat("[OK] Loaded results from RData file\n")
  } else {
    stop("[ERROR] Could not find results. Please run the main analysis first or check file path.")
  }
}

# Robust function to find best overall model
find_best_overall_model <- function(results_list, results_df) {
  cat("Searching for best overall model...\n")
  
  # Print available methods for debugging
  cat("Available methods in results:\n")
  print(unique(results_df$Method))
  
  # Find all successful models (both with and without feature selection)
  successful_models <- results_df[results_df$Success == TRUE, ]
  
  if(nrow(successful_models) == 0) {
    stop("[ERROR] No successful models found")
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("Found %d successful models\n", nrow(successful_models)))
  
  # Show top 10 models for reference
  top_models <- successful_models[order(successful_models$Balanced_Accuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ][1:min(10, nrow(successful_models)), ]
  cat("Top 10 performing models:\n")
  print(top_models[, c("Taxonomic_Level", "Method", "Feature_Selection", "Balanced_Accuracy")])
  
  # Get the best performing one overall
  best_model <- successful_models[which.max(successful_models$Balanced_Accuracy), ]
  cat(sprintf("[OK] Best overall model: %s (%s) at %s level (Balanced Accuracy: %.3f)\n", 
              best_model$Method, best_model$Feature_Selection, best_model$Taxonomic_Level, best_model$Balanced_Accuracy))
  
  # Find the corresponding model object - try multiple naming conventions
  method_clean <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z]", "", best_model$Method)
  fs_suffix <- ifelse(best_model$Feature_Selection == "With FS", "with_fs", "without_fs")
  
  possible_keys <- c(
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_", tolower(method_clean), "_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_svmRadial_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_rf_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_glm_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_knn_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_nb_", fs_suffix),
    paste0(best_model$Taxonomic_Level, "_svmLinear_", fs_suffix)
  )
  
  model_obj <- NULL
  model_key <- NULL
  
  for(key in possible_keys) {
    if(key %in% names(results_list)) {
      model_obj <- results_list[[key]]
      model_key <- key
      cat(sprintf("[OK] Found model object with key: %s\n", key))
      break
    }
  }
  
  if(is.null(model_obj)) {
    cat("Available model keys (first 20):\n")
    cat(paste(head(names(results_list), 20), collapse = ", "), "...\n")
    
    # Try a more flexible search
    cat("Trying flexible search...\n")
    tax_level <- best_model$Taxonomic_Level
    matching_keys <- names(results_list)[grepl(tax_level, names(results_list))]
    cat(sprintf("Keys containing '%s': %s\n", tax_level, paste(head(matching_keys, 10), collapse = ", ")))
    
    # If still not found, try the first matching key
    if(length(matching_keys) > 0) {
      model_obj <- results_list[[matching_keys[1]]]
      model_key <- matching_keys[1]
      cat(sprintf("[WARNING] Using first matching key: %s\n", model_key))
    } else {
      stop("[ERROR] Could not find the model object for best model")
    }
  }
  
  return(list(
    best_result = best_model,
    model_object = model_obj,
    model_key = model_key
  ))
}

best_model_info <- find_best_overall_model(all_results, results_summary)
best_model_result <- best_model_info$best_result

# Verify we have the right model information
if(is.null(best_model_info$best_result)) {
  stop("[ERROR] Failed to identify best model")
}

cat(sprintf("[OK] Using best model: %s (%s) at %s level\n", 
            best_model_info$best_result$Method,
            best_model_info$best_result$Feature_Selection,
            best_model_info$best_result$Taxonomic_Level))

cat("\n=== BEST OVERALL MODEL INFORMATION ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Taxonomic Level: %s\n", best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level))
cat(sprintf("Method: %s\n", best_model_result$Method))
cat(sprintf("Feature Selection: %s\n", best_model_result$Feature_Selection))
cat(sprintf("Balanced Accuracy: %.3f\n", best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy))
cat(sprintf("Accuracy: %.3f\n", best_model_result$Accuracy))
cat(sprintf("Sensitivity: %.3f\n", best_model_result$Sensitivity))
cat(sprintf("Specificity: %.3f\n", best_model_result$Specificity))

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 2. Extract Feature Data and Model Information (Robust)
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== EXTRACTING MODEL DATA ===\n")

# Get the classifier object
model_obj <- best_model_info$model_object
cat("Model object structure:\n")
cat(sprintf("- Class: %s\n", class(model_obj)))
cat(sprintf("- Names: %s\n", paste(names(model_obj), collapse = ", ")))

# Try to get the classifier
classifier <- NULL
if(!is.null(model_obj$model)) {
  classifier <- model_obj$model
} else if(!is.null(model_obj$classifier)) {
  classifier <- model_obj$classifier
} else {
  classifier <- model_obj
}

cat("Classifier object structure:\n")
cat(sprintf("- Class: %s\n", class(classifier)))
cat(sprintf("- Names: %s\n", paste(names(classifier), collapse = ", ")))

# Extract feature matrix with multiple fallback options
feature_matrix <- NULL
response_var <- NULL

# Try different data locations
data_locations <- list(
  list(features = "data_feature", response = "data_response"),
  list(features = "data_train", response = "data_train_y"),
  list(features = "x_train", response = "y_train"),
  list(features = "train_x", response = "train_y")
)

for(location in data_locations) {
  if(!is.null(classifier[[location$features]]) && !is.null(classifier[[location$response]])) {
    feature_matrix <- as.matrix(classifier[[location$features]])
    response_var <- classifier[[location$response]]
    cat(sprintf("[OK] Found data at: %s & %s\n", location$features, location$response))
    break
  }
}

# If still no data found, try to extract from the original dataset
if(is.null(feature_matrix) || is.null(response_var)) {
  cat("Trying to extract from original dataset...\n")
  
  if(exists("meco_dataset")) {
    # Recreate the feature matrix from the original data
    tax_level <- best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level
    
    # Try to recreate classifier to get the data
    tryCatch({
      temp_classifier <- trans_classifier$new(
        dataset = meco_dataset, 
        y.response = "BeforeMalaria", 
        x.predictors = tax_level
      )
      temp_classifier$cal_split(prop.train = 3/4)
      
      # Use all data (train + test)
      feature_matrix <- rbind(temp_classifier$data_train, temp_classifier$data_test %||% data.frame())
      response_var <- c(temp_classifier$data_train_y, temp_classifier$data_test_y %||% factor())
      
      cat("[OK] Recreated feature matrix from original dataset\n")
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("[ERROR] Failed to recreate data: %s\n", e$message))
    })
  }
}

if(is.null(feature_matrix) || is.null(response_var)) {
  stop("[ERROR] Cannot extract feature matrix and response variable")
}

# Clean and validate data
feature_matrix <- as.matrix(feature_matrix)
response_var <- as.factor(response_var)

cat(sprintf("[OK] Feature matrix: %d samples × %d features\n", nrow(feature_matrix), ncol(feature_matrix)))
cat(sprintf("[OK] Response levels: %s\n", paste(levels(response_var), collapse = ", ")))

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 3. Simple Feature Importance Methods
## --------------------------------------------------------------------

# Method 1: Correlation-based importance (Always works)
calculate_correlation_importance <- function(feature_matrix, response_var) {
  cat("Calculating correlation-based feature importance...\n")
  
  # Convert response to numeric
  response_numeric <- as.numeric(response_var) - 1
  
  # Calculate correlations
  correlations <- apply(feature_matrix, 2, function(x) {
    cor(x, response_numeric, use = "complete.obs")
  })
  
  # Remove any NA correlations
  correlations[is.na(correlations)] <- 0
  
  importance_df <- data.frame(
    Feature = names(correlations),
    Correlation = abs(correlations),
    Raw_Correlation = correlations,
    Importance = abs(correlations),  # Use absolute correlation as importance
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  importance_df <- importance_df[order(importance_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  cat(sprintf("[OK] Calculated correlations for %d features\n", nrow(importance_df)))
  return(importance_df)
}

# Method 2: Variance-based importance
calculate_variance_importance <- function(feature_matrix, response_var) {
  cat("Calculating variance-based feature importance...\n")
  
  groups <- levels(response_var)
  group1_idx <- which(response_var == groups[1])
  group2_idx <- which(response_var == groups[2])
  
  variance_ratios <- apply(feature_matrix, 2, function(x) {
    var1 <- var(x[group1_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
    var2 <- var(x[group2_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
    mean1 <- mean(x[group1_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
    mean2 <- mean(x[group2_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Calculate effect size (Cohen's d)
    pooled_sd <- sqrt(((length(group1_idx) - 1) * var1 + (length(group2_idx) - 1) * var2) / 
                        (length(group1_idx) + length(group2_idx) - 2))
    effect_size <- abs(mean1 - mean2) / pooled_sd
    
    return(effect_size)
  })
  
  variance_ratios[is.na(variance_ratios)] <- 0
  
  variance_df <- data.frame(
    Feature = names(variance_ratios),
    Effect_Size = variance_ratios,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  variance_df <- variance_df[order(variance_df$Effect_Size, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  cat(sprintf("[OK] Calculated effect sizes for %d features\n", nrow(variance_df)))
  return(variance_df)
}

# Method 3: Wilcoxon test-based importance
calculate_wilcoxon_importance <- function(feature_matrix, response_var) {
  cat("Calculating Wilcoxon test-based feature importance...\n")
  
  groups <- levels(response_var)
  group1_idx <- which(response_var == groups[1])
  group2_idx <- which(response_var == groups[2])
  
  wilcox_results <- apply(feature_matrix, 2, function(x) {
    tryCatch({
      test_result <- wilcox.test(x[group1_idx], x[group2_idx])
      # Use -log10(p-value) as importance, capped at 10
      importance <- min(-log10(test_result$p.value + 1e-10), 10)
      return(c(p_value = test_result$p.value, importance = importance))
    }, error = function(e) {
      return(c(p_value = 1, importance = 0))
    })
  })
  
  wilcox_df <- data.frame(
    Feature = colnames(feature_matrix),
    P_Value = wilcox_results[1, ],
    Wilcox_Importance = wilcox_results[2, ],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  wilcox_df <- wilcox_df[order(wilcox_df$Wilcox_Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  cat(sprintf("[OK] Calculated Wilcoxon importance for %d features\n", nrow(wilcox_df)))
  return(wilcox_df)
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 4. Run Feature Importance Analyses
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== CALCULATING FEATURE IMPORTANCE ===\n")

# Calculate all importance measures
correlation_importance <- calculate_correlation_importance(feature_matrix, response_var)
variance_importance <- calculate_variance_importance(feature_matrix, response_var)
wilcoxon_importance <- calculate_wilcoxon_importance(feature_matrix, response_var)

# Combine all measures
combined_importance <- correlation_importance
combined_importance <- merge(combined_importance, variance_importance, by = "Feature", all.x = TRUE)
combined_importance <- merge(combined_importance, wilcoxon_importance, by = "Feature", all.x = TRUE)

# Create composite score
combined_importance$Composite_Score <- 
  scale(combined_importance$Importance)[,1] + 
  scale(combined_importance$Effect_Size)[,1] + 
  scale(combined_importance$Wilcox_Importance)[,1]

combined_importance <- combined_importance[order(combined_importance$Composite_Score, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Select top features
top_n_features <- min(20, nrow(combined_importance))
top_features <- combined_importance[1:top_n_features, ]

cat(sprintf("\n=== TOP %d MOST IMPORTANT FEATURES ===\n", top_n_features))
print(top_features[1:min(10, nrow(top_features)), 
                   c("Feature", "Importance", "Effect_Size", "P_Value", "Composite_Score")])

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 5. Create Visualizations
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== CREATING VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")

# Create output directory
dir.create("SixWeekResults", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Figure 1: Feature Importance Plot
create_importance_plot <- function(importance_data, top_n = 15) {
  plot_data <- importance_data[1:min(top_n, nrow(importance_data)), ]
  
  # Clean feature names
  plot_data$Feature_Clean <- gsub(".*__", "", plot_data$Feature)
  plot_data$Feature_Clean <- gsub("_", " ", plot_data$Feature_Clean)
  
  # Create significance categories
  plot_data$Significance <- ifelse(plot_data$P_Value < 0.001, "p < 0.001",
                                   ifelse(plot_data$P_Value < 0.01, "p < 0.01",
                                          ifelse(plot_data$P_Value < 0.05, "p < 0.05", "p ≥ 0.05")))
  
  p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Feature_Clean, Composite_Score), y = Composite_Score)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = Significance), alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("p < 0.001" = "#E74C3C", "p < 0.01" = "#F39C12", 
                                 "p < 0.05" = "#F7DC6F", "p ≥ 0.05" = "#BDC3C7")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("Top %d Most Important Features for Malaria History Classification", top_n),
      subtitle = sprintf("%s - %s Level (%s) - Balanced Accuracy: %.1f%%", 
                         best_model_result$Method, best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level, 
                         best_model_result$Feature_Selection, best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy * 100),
      x = "Microbial Features",
      y = "Composite Importance Score",
      fill = "Statistical Significance",
      caption = "Importance based on correlation, effect size, and Wilcoxon test"
    )
  
  return(p1)
}

importance_plot <- create_importance_plot(combined_importance, top_n = 15)
ggsave("SixWeekResults/feature_importance_plot.pdf", importance_plot, 
       width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("SixWeekResults/feature_importance_plot.png", importance_plot, 
       width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

cat("[OK] Saved feature importance plot\n")

# Figure 2: Box plots for top features
create_boxplots <- function(feature_matrix, response_var, top_features_df, top_n = 6) {
  top_feature_names <- top_features_df$Feature[1:min(top_n, nrow(top_features_df))]
  
  plot_list <- list()
  
  for(i in 1:length(top_feature_names)) {
    feature_name <- top_feature_names[i]
    clean_name <- gsub(".*__", "", feature_name)
    clean_name <- gsub("_", " ", clean_name)
    
    plot_data <- data.frame(
      Abundance = feature_matrix[, feature_name],
      Group = response_var
    )
    
    # Get p-value for this feature
    p_val <- top_features_df$P_Value[top_features_df$Feature == feature_name]
    p_text <- ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "p < 0.001", sprintf("p = %.3f", p_val))
    
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Group, y = Abundance, fill = Group)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Yes" = "#E74C3C", "Never" = "#2E86C1")) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
      ) +
      labs(
        title = clean_name,
        x = "Previous Malaria",
        y = "Relative Abundance"
      ) +
      annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = max(plot_data$Abundance) * 0.9, 
               label = p_text, size = 3, fontface = "italic")
    
    plot_list[[i]] <- p
  }
  
  # Combine plots
  combined_plot <- do.call(grid.arrange, c(plot_list, ncol = 3))
  
  return(combined_plot)
}

boxplot_figure <- create_boxplots(feature_matrix, response_var, combined_importance, top_n = 6)
ggsave("SixWeekResults/top_features_boxplots.pdf", boxplot_figure, 
       width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("SixWeekResults/top_features_boxplots.png", boxplot_figure, 
       width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 300)

cat("[OK] Saved boxplot figure\n")

# Figure 3: Heatmap (if pheatmap is available)
tryCatch({
  if(requireNamespace("pheatmap", quietly = TRUE)) {
    top_feature_names <- combined_importance$Feature[1:min(15, nrow(combined_importance))]
    heatmap_data <- feature_matrix[, top_feature_names, drop = FALSE]
    
    # Clean feature names
    colnames(heatmap_data) <- gsub(".*__", "", colnames(heatmap_data))
    colnames(heatmap_data) <- gsub("_", " ", colnames(heatmap_data))
    
    # Create annotation
    sample_annotation <- data.frame(
      Malaria_History = response_var,
      row.names = rownames(heatmap_data)
    )
    
    annotation_colors <- list(
      Malaria_History = c("Yes" = "#E74C3C", "Never" = "#2E86C1")
    )
    
    pheatmap::pheatmap(
      t(heatmap_data),
      annotation_col = sample_annotation,
      annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
      scale = "row",
      clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
      clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
      color = colorRampPalette(c("#2E86C1", "white", "#E74C3C"))(100),
      main = sprintf("Top 15 Features - Abundance Heatmap\n%s Level (%s)", 
                     best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level, best_model_result$Method),
      fontsize = 10,
      fontsize_row = 9,
      fontsize_col = 8,
      angle_col = 90,
      filename = "SixWeekResults/abundance_heatmap.pdf",
      width = 14,
      height = 8
    )
    # --- SAVE AS PNG  ---
    pheatmap::pheatmap(
      t(heatmap_data),
      annotation_col = sample_annotation,
      annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
      scale = "row",
      clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
      clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
      color = colorRampPalette(c("#2E86C1", "white", "#E74C3C"))(100),
      main = sprintf("Top 15 Features - Abundance Heatmap\n%s Level (%s)", 
                     best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level, best_model_result$Method),
      fontsize = 10,
      fontsize_row = 9,
      fontsize_col = 8,
      angle_col = 90,
      filename = "SixWeekResults/abundance_heatmap.png", # Changed extension to png
      width = 14,
      height = 8
    )
    cat("[OK] Saved heatmap\n")
  }
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("[WARNING] Could not create heatmap (pheatmap not available)\n")
})

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 6. Save Results
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SAVING RESULTS ===\n")

# Save feature importance results
write.csv(combined_importance, "SixWeekResults/feature_importance_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save top features data
top_features_data <- feature_matrix[, combined_importance$Feature[1:min(20, nrow(combined_importance))]]
top_features_with_response <- cbind(response_var, top_features_data)
write.csv(top_features_with_response, "SixWeekResults/top_features_data.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Save model information
model_info <- data.frame(
  Attribute = c("Taxonomic_Level", "Method", "Feature_Selection", "Balanced_Accuracy", 
                "Accuracy", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Total_Features"),
  Value = c(best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level, best_model_result$Method, 
            best_model_result$Feature_Selection, best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy,
            best_model_result$Accuracy, best_model_result$Sensitivity, 
            best_model_result$Specificity, ncol(feature_matrix)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
write.csv(model_info, "SixWeekResults/best_model_info.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("[OK] Saved CSV files\n")

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. ROC Curve Analysis
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== GENERATING ROC CURVE ===\n")

# Extract the classifier object from the best model
best_classifier <- NULL

# Try to get the classifier object
if(!is.null(best_model_info$model_object$model)) {
  best_classifier <- best_model_info$model_object$model
  cat("[OK] Found classifier in model_object$model\n")
} else if(!is.null(best_model_info$model_object)) {
  best_classifier <- best_model_info$model_object
  cat("[OK] Using model_object directly as classifier\n")
} else {
  cat("[ERROR] Could not find classifier object\n")
}

if(!is.null(best_classifier)) {
  tryCatch({
    # Check if ROC has already been calculated
    if(is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC)) {
      cat("Calculating ROC curve...\n")
      # Calculate ROC using training data
      best_classifier$cal_ROC(input = "train")
      cat("[OK] ROC calculation completed\n")
    } else {
      cat("[OK] ROC already calculated\n")
    }
    
    # Plot ROC curve
    cat("Creating ROC plot...\n")
    roc_plot <- best_classifier$plot_ROC(plot_type = "ROC", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.7)
    
    # Enhance the ROC plot with better labels
    roc_plot_enhanced <- roc_plot + 
      labs(
        title = sprintf("ROC Curve - Best Model Performance"),
        subtitle = sprintf("%s (%s) at %s Level - Balanced Accuracy: %.1f%%", 
                           best_model_result$Method, 
                           best_model_result$Feature_Selection,
                           best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level, 
                           best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy * 100),
        caption = sprintf("AUC: %.3f | Sensitivity: %.3f | Specificity: %.3f", 
                          ifelse(!is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC$auc), best_classifier$res_ROC$auc, 0),
                          best_model_result$Sensitivity,
                          best_model_result$Specificity)
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5)
      )
    
    # Save ROC plot
    ggsave("SixWeekResults/roc_curve.pdf", roc_plot_enhanced, 
           width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    ggsave("SixWeekResults/roc_curve.png", roc_plot_enhanced, 
           width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    
    cat("[OK] ROC curve saved as roc_curve.pdf and roc_curve.png\n")
    
    # Extract AUC value if available
    if(!is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC)) {
      if(!is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC$auc)) {
        auc_value <- best_classifier$res_ROC$auc
        cat(sprintf("[OK] AUC: %.3f\n", auc_value))
      } else {
        cat("[WARNING] AUC value not found in ROC results\n")
      }
    }
    
    # Display the plot
    print(roc_plot_enhanced)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("[ERROR] Error creating ROC curve: %s\n", e$message))
    cat("This might happen if:\n")
    cat("- The model doesn't support ROC analysis\n")
    cat("- Training data is not available\n")
    cat("- The classifier object structure is different\n")
    
    # Alternative: Try to recalculate ROC from scratch
    cat("\nTrying alternative ROC calculation...\n")
    
    tryCatch({
      # Recreate classifier if needed
      if(exists("meco_dataset")) {
        temp_classifier <- trans_classifier$new(
          dataset = meco_dataset, 
          y.response = "BeforeMalaria", 
          x.predictors = best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level
        )
        temp_classifier$cal_split(prop.train = 3/4)
        
        # Apply feature selection if the best model used it
        if(best_model_result$Feature_Selection == "With FS") {
          temp_classifier$cal_feature_sel(boruta.maxRuns = 200, boruta.pValue = 0.01)
        }
        
        # Set training control
        temp_classifier$set_trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, 
                                         savePredictions = TRUE, classProbs = TRUE)
        
        # Train the specific best model
        method_code <- switch(best_model_result$Method,
                              "Random Forest" = "rf",
                              "SVM (Radial Kernel)" = "svmRadial",
                              "SVM (Linear Kernel)" = "svmLinear",
                              "Logistic Regression" = "glm",
                              "k-Nearest Neighbors" = "knn",
                              "Naive Bayes" = "nb",
                              "rf")  # default fallback
        
        temp_classifier$cal_train(method = method_code)
        temp_classifier$cal_predict()
        temp_classifier$cal_ROC(input = "train")
        
        # Plot ROC
        alt_roc_plot <- temp_classifier$plot_ROC(plot_type = "ROC", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.7)
        alt_roc_plot_enhanced <- alt_roc_plot + 
          labs(
            title = "ROC Curve - Best Model (Recreated)",
            subtitle = sprintf("%s (%s) at %s Level", 
                               best_model_result$Method, 
                               best_model_result$Feature_Selection,
                               best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level)
          )
        
        ggsave("SixWeekResults/roc_curve_alt.pdf", alt_roc_plot_enhanced, 
               width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
        
        cat("[OK] Alternative ROC curve created and saved as roc_curve_alt.pdf\n")
        print(alt_roc_plot_enhanced)
        
      }
    }, error = function(e2) {
      cat(sprintf("[ERROR] Alternative ROC calculation also failed: %s\n", e2$message))
    })
  })
} else {
  cat("[ERROR] Cannot create ROC curve - classifier object not found\n")
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. Confusion Matrix Analysis
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== GENERATING CONFUSION MATRIX ===\n")

if(!is.null(best_classifier)) {
  tryCatch({
    # Plot confusion matrix using simple microeco call
    cat("Creating confusion matrix plot...\n")
    confusion_plot <- best_classifier$plot_confusionMatrix()
    
    # Save confusion matrix plot
    ggsave("SixWeekResults/confusion_matrix.pdf", confusion_plot, 
           width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    ggsave("SixWeekResults/confusion_matrix.png", confusion_plot, 
           width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    
    cat("[OK] Confusion matrix saved as confusion_matrix.pdf and confusion_matrix.png\n")
    
    # Display the plot
    print(confusion_plot)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("[ERROR] Error creating confusion matrix: %s\n", e$message))
    cat("The plot_confusionMatrix() method may not be available for this classifier object.\n")
  })
} else {
  cat("[ERROR] Cannot create confusion matrix - classifier object not found\n")
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 9. Additional Performance Summary Table
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== CREATING PERFORMANCE SUMMARY TABLE ===\n")

# Create a comprehensive performance summary
performance_summary <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Balanced Accuracy", "Accuracy", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", 
             "Precision", "F1 Score"),
  Value = c(
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy, best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy * 100),
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", best_model_result$Accuracy, best_model_result$Accuracy * 100),
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", best_model_result$Sensitivity, best_model_result$Sensitivity * 100),
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", best_model_result$Specificity, best_model_result$Specificity * 100),
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", ifelse(is.na(best_model_result$Precision), 0, best_model_result$Precision), 
            ifelse(is.na(best_model_result$Precision), 0, best_model_result$Precision) * 100),
    sprintf("%.3f (%.1f%%)", ifelse(is.na(best_model_result$F1_Score), 0, best_model_result$F1_Score), 
            ifelse(is.na(best_model_result$F1_Score), 0, best_model_result$F1_Score) * 100)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Add AUC if available
if(!is.null(best_classifier) && !is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC) && !is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC$auc)) {
  performance_summary <- rbind(performance_summary,
                               data.frame(Metric = "AUC", 
                                          Value = sprintf("%.3f", best_classifier$res_ROC$auc),
                                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
}

# Save performance summary
write.csv(performance_summary, "SixWeekResults/performance_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("[OK] Performance summary saved as performance_summary.csv\n")
cat("\nPerformance Summary:\n")
print(performance_summary)

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 10. Updated Final Summary Report
## --------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("BEST MODEL FEATURE EXTRACTION AND ANALYSIS - SUMMARY REPORT\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

cat(sprintf("Best Model Details:\n"))
cat(sprintf("- Taxonomic Level: %s\n", best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level))
cat(sprintf("- Method: %s\n", best_model_result$Method))
cat(sprintf("- Feature Selection: %s\n", best_model_result$Feature_Selection))
cat(sprintf("- Balanced Accuracy: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy * 100))
cat(sprintf("- Accuracy: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$Accuracy * 100))
cat(sprintf("- Sensitivity: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$Sensitivity * 100))
cat(sprintf("- Specificity: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$Specificity * 100))
if(!is.na(best_model_result$Precision)) {
  cat(sprintf("- Precision: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$Precision * 100))
}
if(!is.na(best_model_result$F1_Score)) {
  cat(sprintf("- F1 Score: %.1f%%\n", best_model_result$F1_Score * 100))
}
cat(sprintf("- Total Features Used: %d\n", ncol(feature_matrix)))

# Add AUC if available
if(!is.null(best_classifier) && !is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC) && !is.null(best_classifier$res_ROC$auc)) {
  cat(sprintf("- AUC: %.3f\n", best_classifier$res_ROC$auc))
}

cat(sprintf("\nTop 5 Most Important Features:\n"))
for(i in 1:min(5, nrow(combined_importance))) {
  feature_clean <- gsub(".*__", "", combined_importance$Feature[i])
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s (Score: %.3f, p-value: %.3f)\n", 
              i, feature_clean, combined_importance$Composite_Score[i], 
              combined_importance$P_Value[i]))
}

cat(sprintf("\nGenerated Files in SixWeekResults folder:\n"))
cat("=== Visualizations ===\n")
cat("- feature_importance_plot.pdf/png: Main feature importance visualization\n")
cat("- top_features_boxplots.pdf/png: Distribution of top features by group\n")
cat("- abundance_heatmap.pdf: Heatmap of top features across samples\n")
cat("- roc_curve.pdf/png: ROC curve for best model performance\n")
cat("- confusion_matrix.pdf/png: Confusion matrix visualization\n")

cat("\n=== Data Files ===\n")
cat("- feature_importance_results.csv: Complete feature importance data\n")
cat("- top_features_data.csv: Abundance data for top features\n")
cat("- best_model_info.csv: Best model summary information\n")
cat("- performance_summary.csv: Comprehensive performance metrics\n")

cat("\n=== Model Performance Interpretation ===\n")
bal_acc_pct <- best_model_result$Balanced_Accuracy * 100
if(bal_acc_pct >= 85) {
  interpretation <- "Excellent predictive performance - very strong microbiome signal"
} else if(bal_acc_pct >= 75) {
  interpretation <- "Good predictive performance - strong microbiome signal detected"
} else if(bal_acc_pct >= 65) {
  interpretation <- "Moderate predictive performance - detectable microbiome signal"
} else if(bal_acc_pct >= 55) {
  interpretation <- "Fair predictive performance - weak but present microbiome signal"
} else {
  interpretation <- "Limited predictive performance - minimal microbiome signal"
}

cat(sprintf("Performance Level: %s\n", interpretation))

# Add clinical/biological interpretation
cat(sprintf("\nBiological Interpretation:\n"))
cat(sprintf("The %s level taxonomy provides the best discrimination between\n", best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level))
cat(sprintf("individuals with and without previous malaria history (Balanced Accuracy: %.1f%%).\n", bal_acc_pct))
cat(sprintf("This suggests that previous malaria infection leaves detectable signatures\n"))
cat(sprintf("in the gut microbiome at the %s taxonomic resolution.\n", tolower(best_model_result$Taxonomic_Level)))

if(best_model_result$Feature_Selection == "With FS") {
  cat(sprintf("\nFeature selection improved model performance, indicating that\n"))
  cat(sprintf("a subset of specific microbial taxa are most informative for\n"))
  cat(sprintf("distinguishing malaria history rather than the entire microbial community.\n"))
} else {
  cat(sprintf("\nThe model performed best without feature selection, suggesting that\n"))
  cat(sprintf("the entire microbial community profile contributes to the discrimination\n"))
  cat(sprintf("rather than just a few specific taxa.\n"))
}

cat("\n=== COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")

# Show the final importance plot
```


## First-Year Classifier (for completeness)

Verbatim from `FirstYearModelDetermination.R`. As reported in the
Supplemental Methods, this model failed at the test set step.

```{r classifier-1y, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(123)
dir.create("FirstYear", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Data Preparation for First Year (verbatim, readRDS replaced)
meco_dataset_1y <- phyloseq2meco(combinedcongo)
meco_dataset_1y$tidy_dataset()
meco_dataset_1y$tax_table <- base::subset(meco_dataset_1y$tax_table, Kingdom %in% c("k__Archaea", "k__Bacteria"))
meco_dataset_1y$filter_pollution(taxa = c("mitochondria", "chloroplast"))
st_1y <- subset(meco_dataset_1y$sample_table, Type == "sample")
st_1y$BeforeMalaria <- as.character(st_1y$BeforeMalaria)
st_1y$BeforeMalaria[is.na(st_1y$BeforeMalaria)] <- "Never"
st_1y <- subset(st_1y, BeforeMalaria %in% c("Yes", "Never"))
st_1y$BeforeMalaria <- factor(st_1y$BeforeMalaria, levels = c("Yes", "Never"))
meco_dataset_1y$sample_table <- st_1y
meco_dataset_1y$tidy_dataset()

cat(sprintf("First-year dataset: %d samples\n", nrow(meco_dataset_1y$sample_table)))
print(table(meco_dataset_1y$sample_table$BeforeMalaria))
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 2. Determine Available Taxonomic Levels
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
get_available_taxonomic_levels <- function(meco_obj) {
  # Get all taxonomic columns from tax_table
  tax_cols <- colnames(meco_obj$tax_table)
  
  # Remove 'Taxa' column if it exists (this is typically the row identifier)
  tax_cols <- tax_cols[tax_cols != "Taxa"]
  
  # Common taxonomic level mappings
  level_mappings <- c(
    "Kingdom" = "Kingdom",
    "Phylum" = "Phylum", 
    "Class" = "Class",
    "Order" = "Order",
    "Family" = "Family",
    "Genus" = "Genus",
    "Species" = "Species"
  )
  
  # Find which levels are available
  available_levels <- c()
  for(level in names(level_mappings)) {
    if(level %in% tax_cols) {
      available_levels <- c(available_levels, level)
    }
  }
  
  # If no standard names found, use all columns except Taxa
  if(length(available_levels) == 0) {
    available_levels <- tax_cols
    cat("Warning: Using all tax_table columns as taxonomic levels\n")
  }
  
  return(available_levels)
}

# Get available taxonomic levels
taxonomic_levels <- get_available_taxonomic_levels(meco_dataset)
cat("Available taxonomic levels:\n")
cat(paste(taxonomic_levels, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 3. Robust Performance Extraction Function
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_microeco_performance <- function(classifier_obj) {
  results <- list(
    accuracy = NA,
    sensitivity = NA,
    specificity = NA,
    precision = NA,
    f1_score = NA,
    balanced_accuracy = NA,
    confusion_matrix = NULL,
    success = FALSE
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    # Check multiple possible locations for confusion matrix
    cm <- NULL
    
    # Method 1: res_predict$confusionMatrix
    if(!is.null(classifier_obj$res_predict) && is.list(classifier_obj$res_predict)) {
      if("confusionMatrix" %in% names(classifier_obj$res_predict)) {
        cm <- classifier_obj$res_predict$confusionMatrix
      }
    }
    
    # Method 2: Direct access to confusionMatrix
    if(is.null(cm) && !is.null(classifier_obj$res_confusion_fit)) {
      cm <- classifier_obj$res_confusion_fit
    }
    
    # Method 3: Check res_confusion_stats
    if(is.null(cm) && !is.null(classifier_obj$res_confusion_stats)) {
      cm <- classifier_obj$res_confusion_stats
    }
    
    if(!is.null(cm)) {
      # Extract overall metrics
      if(!is.null(cm$overall) && is.vector(cm$overall)) {
        if("Accuracy" %in% names(cm$overall)) {
          results$accuracy <- as.numeric(cm$overall["Accuracy"])
        }
      }
      
      # Extract by-class metrics
      if(!is.null(cm$byClass) && is.vector(cm$byClass)) {
        metric_names <- names(cm$byClass)
        
        if("Sensitivity" %in% metric_names) {
          results$sensitivity <- as.numeric(cm$byClass["Sensitivity"])
        }
        if("Specificity" %in% metric_names) {
          results$specificity <- as.numeric(cm$byClass["Specificity"])
        }
        if("Pos Pred Value" %in% metric_names) {
          results$precision <- as.numeric(cm$byClass["Pos Pred Value"])
        }
        if("F1" %in% metric_names) {
          results$f1_score <- as.numeric(cm$byClass["F1"])
        }
        if("Balanced Accuracy" %in% metric_names) {
          results$balanced_accuracy <- as.numeric(cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"])
        }
        
        # Calculate balanced accuracy if not available
        if(is.na(results$balanced_accuracy) && !is.na(results$sensitivity) && !is.na(results$specificity)) {
          results$balanced_accuracy <- (results$sensitivity + results$specificity) / 2
        }
      }
      
      results$confusion_matrix <- cm
      results$success <- TRUE
      
      cat(sprintf("  Performance extracted: Accuracy=%.3f, Sensitivity=%.3f, Specificity=%.3f\n",
                  ifelse(is.na(results$accuracy), 0, results$accuracy),
                  ifelse(is.na(results$sensitivity), 0, results$sensitivity),
                  ifelse(is.na(results$specificity), 0, results$specificity)))
    } else {
      cat("  Warning: No confusion matrix found in classifier object\n")
    }
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("  Error extracting performance: %s\n", e$message))
  })
  
  return(results)
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 4. Enhanced Single Classifier Training Function
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
train_microeco_classifier <- function(dataset, method_name, method_display_name, 
                                      taxonomic_level, with_feature_selection = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) {
  
  if(verbose) cat(sprintf("\nTraining %s (%s) at %s level%s...\n", 
                          method_display_name, method_name, taxonomic_level,
                          ifelse(with_feature_selection, " with Feature Selection", "")))
  
  result <- list(
    method_code = method_name,
    method_name = method_display_name,
    taxonomic_level = taxonomic_level,
    with_fs = with_feature_selection,
    model = NULL,
    performance = NULL,
    success = FALSE,
    error_message = NULL
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    # Create classifier
    classifier <- trans_classifier$new(
      dataset = dataset, 
      y.response = "BeforeMalaria", 
      x.predictors = taxonomic_level  # Now using the specified taxonomic level
    )
    
    # Check if we have enough features to proceed
    if(!is.null(classifier$data_feature)) {
      initial_feature_count <- ncol(classifier$data_feature)
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("  Available features at %s level: %d\n", taxonomic_level, initial_feature_count))
      
      # Skip if too few features for meaningful analysis
      if(initial_feature_count < 2) {
        stop(sprintf("Insufficient features (%d) at %s level for classification", initial_feature_count, taxonomic_level))
      }
    }
    
    # Split data
    classifier$cal_split(prop.train = 3/4)
    
    # Feature selection if requested
    if(with_feature_selection) {
      # Check number of features available
      initial_features <- ncol(classifier$data_feature)
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("  Initial features available: %d\n", initial_features))
      
      # Skip feature selection if too few features
      if(initial_features <= 3) {
        if(verbose) cat(sprintf("  Skipping feature selection (only %d features available)\n", initial_features))
      } else {
        classifier$cal_feature_sel(boruta.maxRuns = 200, boruta.pValue = 0.01)
        
        # Check if any features were selected
        remaining_features <- ncol(classifier$data_train)
        if(verbose) cat(sprintf("  Features after selection: %d\n", remaining_features))
        
        # If no features selected, skip this model
        if(remaining_features == 0) {
          stop("No features selected by Boruta - model cannot be trained")
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Set training control
    classifier$set_trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, savePredictions = TRUE, classProbs = TRUE)
    
    # Train model with method-specific parameters
    if(method_name == "rf") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "rf")
    } else if(method_name == "svmRadial") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "svmRadial", tuneLength = 5)
    } else if(method_name == "svmLinear") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "svmLinear", tuneLength = 3)
    } else if(method_name == "glm") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "glm")
    } else if(method_name == "knn") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "knn", tuneGrid = expand.grid(k = c(3, 5, 7)))
    } else if(method_name == "nb") {
      classifier$cal_train(method = "nb")
    } else {
      classifier$cal_train(method = method_name)
    }
    
    # Make predictions
    classifier$cal_predict()
    
    # Calculate ROC (optional, with error handling)
    tryCatch({
      classifier$cal_ROC()
    }, error = function(e) {
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("  Note: ROC calculation failed: %s\n", e$message))
    })
    
    # Store model
    result$model <- classifier
    
    # Extract performance
    performance <- extract_microeco_performance(classifier)
    result$performance <- performance
    result$success <- performance$success
    
    if(result$success) {
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("[OK] %s (%s) training completed successfully\n", method_display_name, taxonomic_level))
    } else {
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("[WARNING] %s (%s) training completed but performance extraction failed\n", method_display_name, taxonomic_level))
    }
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    result$error_message <- e$message
    if(verbose) cat(sprintf("[FAIL] Error training %s (%s): %s\n", method_display_name, taxonomic_level, e$message))
  })
  
  return(result)
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 5. Main Analysis Pipeline
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== COMPREHENSIVE MULTI-TAXONOMIC MICROECO CLASSIFIER ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Dataset info:\n")
cat(sprintf("- Total samples: %d\n", nrow(meco_dataset$sample_table)))
cat(sprintf("- Response variable levels: %s\n", paste(levels(meco_dataset$sample_table$BeforeMalaria), collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("- Total features available: %d\n", nrow(meco_dataset$otu_table)))
cat(sprintf("- Taxonomic levels to analyze: %s\n", paste(taxonomic_levels, collapse = ", ")))

# Define methods to test
methods_to_test <- list(
  "rf" = "Random Forest",
  "svmRadial" = "SVM (Radial Kernel)",
  "svmLinear" = "SVM (Linear Kernel)", 
  "glm" = "Logistic Regression",
  "knn" = "k-Nearest Neighbors",
  "nb" = "Naive Bayes"
)

# Storage for all results
all_results <- list()

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 6. Run All Classifiers for All Taxonomic Levels
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== TRAINING ALL CLASSIFIERS ACROSS ALL TAXONOMIC LEVELS ===\n")

total_combinations <- length(taxonomic_levels) * length(methods_to_test) * 2  # *2 for with/without FS
current_combination <- 0

for(tax_level in taxonomic_levels) {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- ANALYZING TAXONOMIC LEVEL: %s ---\n", tax_level))
  
  # Quick check of feature availability at this level
  tryCatch({
    temp_classifier <- trans_classifier$new(
      dataset = meco_dataset, 
      y.response = "BeforeMalaria", 
      x.predictors = tax_level
    )
    feature_count <- ncol(temp_classifier$data_feature)
    cat(sprintf("Features available at %s level: %d\n", tax_level, feature_count))
    
    if(feature_count < 2) {
      cat(sprintf("[WARNING] Skipping %s level - insufficient features for classification\n", tax_level))
      # Still increment counters to maintain progress tracking
      current_combination <- current_combination + (length(methods_to_test) * 2)
      next
    }
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("[WARNING] Error checking %s level: %s\n", tax_level, e$message))
    current_combination <- current_combination + (length(methods_to_test) * 2)
    next
  })
  
  for(method_code in names(methods_to_test)) {
    method_name <- methods_to_test[[method_code]]
    
    # With feature selection
    current_combination <- current_combination + 1
    cat(sprintf("\n[%d/%d] ", current_combination, total_combinations))
    
    result_with_fs <- train_microeco_classifier(
      dataset = meco_dataset,
      method_name = method_code,
      method_display_name = method_name,
      taxonomic_level = tax_level,
      with_feature_selection = TRUE
    )
    all_results[[paste0(tax_level, "_", method_code, "_with_fs")]] <- result_with_fs
    
    # Without feature selection
    current_combination <- current_combination + 1
    cat(sprintf("\n[%d/%d] ", current_combination, total_combinations))
    
    result_without_fs <- train_microeco_classifier(
      dataset = meco_dataset,
      method_name = method_code,
      method_display_name = method_name,
      taxonomic_level = tax_level,
      with_feature_selection = FALSE
    )
    all_results[[paste0(tax_level, "_", method_code, "_without_fs")]] <- result_without_fs
    
    # Brief pause
    Sys.sleep(1)
  }
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. Create Comprehensive Results Summary
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== CREATING RESULTS SUMMARY ===\n")

create_results_dataframe <- function(results_list) {
  df <- data.frame(
    Taxonomic_Level = character(),
    Method = character(),
    Feature_Selection = character(),
    Success = logical(),
    Accuracy = numeric(),
    Sensitivity = numeric(),
    Specificity = numeric(),
    Precision = numeric(),
    F1_Score = numeric(),
    Balanced_Accuracy = numeric(),
    Error_Message = character(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for(result_name in names(results_list)) {
    result <- results_list[[result_name]]
    
    # Extract performance metrics
    acc <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$accuracy), 
                  result$performance$accuracy, 0)
    sens <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$sensitivity), 
                   result$performance$sensitivity, 0)
    spec <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$specificity), 
                   result$performance$specificity, 0)
    prec <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$precision), 
                   result$performance$precision, 0)
    f1 <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$f1_score), 
                 result$performance$f1_score, 0)
    bal_acc <- ifelse(!is.null(result$performance) && !is.na(result$performance$balanced_accuracy), 
                      result$performance$balanced_accuracy, 0)
    
    df <- rbind(df, data.frame(
      Taxonomic_Level = result$taxonomic_level,
      Method = result$method_name,
      Feature_Selection = ifelse(result$with_fs, "With FS", "Without FS"),
      Success = result$success,
      Accuracy = acc,
      Sensitivity = sens,
      Specificity = spec,
      Precision = prec,
      F1_Score = f1,
      Balanced_Accuracy = bal_acc,
      Error_Message = ifelse(is.null(result$error_message), "", result$error_message),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  return(df)
}

# Create summary dataframe
results_summary <- create_results_dataframe(all_results)

# Print summary by taxonomic level
cat("\n=== PERFORMANCE SUMMARY BY TAXONOMIC LEVEL ===\n")
for(tax_level in taxonomic_levels) {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s Level ---\n", tax_level))
  level_results <- results_summary[results_summary$Taxonomic_Level == tax_level, ]
  print(level_results[, c("Method", "Feature_Selection", "Success", "Accuracy", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Balanced_Accuracy")])
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. Identify Best Models
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== BEST MODEL IDENTIFICATION ===\n")

successful_results <- results_summary[results_summary$Success == TRUE, ]

if(nrow(successful_results) > 0) {
  # Sort by balanced accuracy
  successful_results <- successful_results[order(successful_results$Balanced_Accuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  cat("Top 10 performing models across all taxonomic levels:\n")
  top_n <- min(10, nrow(successful_results))
  for(i in 1:top_n) {
    cat(sprintf("%d. %s - %s (%s): Balanced Accuracy = %.3f, Accuracy = %.3f\n", 
                i, 
                successful_results$Taxonomic_Level[i],
                successful_results$Method[i],
                successful_results$Feature_Selection[i],
                successful_results$Balanced_Accuracy[i],
                successful_results$Accuracy[i]))
  }
  
  # Best overall model
  best_model <- successful_results[1, ]
  cat(sprintf("\n>>> BEST OVERALL MODEL: %s - %s (%s)\n", 
              best_model$Taxonomic_Level, best_model$Method, best_model$Feature_Selection))
  cat(sprintf("   Performance: Accuracy=%.3f, Sensitivity=%.3f, Specificity=%.3f, Balanced Accuracy=%.3f\n",
              best_model$Accuracy, best_model$Sensitivity, best_model$Specificity, best_model$Balanced_Accuracy))
  
  # Best model for each taxonomic level
  cat("\n=== BEST MODEL PER TAXONOMIC LEVEL ===\n")
  for(tax_level in taxonomic_levels) {
    level_results <- successful_results[successful_results$Taxonomic_Level == tax_level, ]
    if(nrow(level_results) > 0) {
      best_for_level <- level_results[1, ]
      cat(sprintf("%s: %s (%s) - Balanced Accuracy = %.3f\n",
                  tax_level, best_for_level$Method, best_for_level$Feature_Selection, 
                  best_for_level$Balanced_Accuracy))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("%s: No successful models\n", tax_level))
    }
  }
  
} else {
  cat("[WARNING] No successful models found. Check for errors in the analysis.\n")
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 9. Generate Enhanced Visualizations
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== GENERATING VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")

if(nrow(successful_results) > 0) {
  
  # Performance comparison plot by taxonomic level
  library(reshape2)
  plot_data <- successful_results[, c("Taxonomic_Level", "Method", "Feature_Selection", "Accuracy", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Balanced_Accuracy")]
  melted_data <- melt(plot_data, id.vars = c("Taxonomic_Level", "Method", "Feature_Selection"))
  
  performance_plot <- ggplot(melted_data, aes(x = Method, y = value, fill = Feature_Selection)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_grid(variable ~ Taxonomic_Level, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 9)) +
    labs(title = "Classifier Performance Across All Taxonomic Levels",
         subtitle = sprintf("Analysis of %d samples with %s outcome classification", 
                            nrow(meco_dataset$sample_table),
                            paste(levels(meco_dataset$sample_table$BeforeMalaria), collapse = " vs ")),
         x = "Classification Method",
         y = "Performance Metric",
         fill = "Feature Selection") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("With FS" = "#2E86C1", "Without FS" = "#E74C3C")) +
    ylim(0, 1)
  
  ggsave("FirstYear/FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_performance.pdf", performance_plot, width = 20, height = 12, dpi = 300)
  cat("[OK] Multi-taxonomic performance comparison plot saved to FirstYear folder\n")
  
  # Summary heatmap showing best performance per taxonomic level
  summary_for_heatmap <- successful_results %>%
    group_by(Taxonomic_Level, Method) %>%
    summarise(Best_Balanced_Accuracy = max(Balanced_Accuracy), .groups = 'drop')
  
  heatmap_plot <- ggplot(summary_for_heatmap, aes(x = Method, y = Taxonomic_Level, fill = Best_Balanced_Accuracy)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", Best_Balanced_Accuracy)), color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#E74C3C", mid = "#F39C12", high = "#27AE60", 
                         midpoint = 0.6, name = "Balanced\nAccuracy") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = "Best Performance Heatmap by Taxonomic Level and Method",
         subtitle = "Values shown are best balanced accuracy (with or without feature selection)",
         x = "Classification Method",
         y = "Taxonomic Level")
  
  ggsave("FirstYear/FirstYearmicrobiome_performance_heatmap.pdf", heatmap_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  cat("[OK] Performance heatmap saved to FirstYear folder\n")
  
}

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 10. Save All Results
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SAVING RESULTS ===\n")

# Save summary table to FirstYear folder
write.csv(results_summary, "FirstYear/FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("[OK] Results summary saved to FirstYear folder\n")

# Save R workspace to root folder
save(all_results, results_summary, meco_dataset, successful_results, taxonomic_levels,
     file = "FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")
cat("[OK] Complete analysis saved as RData file in root folder\n")

## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## 11. Final Summary Report
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("MULTI-TAXONOMIC MICROBIOME CLASSIFICATION ANALYSIS - FINAL REPORT\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

cat(sprintf("Dataset: Congo Malaria Microbiome Study\n"))
cat(sprintf("Target Variable: Before Malaria (Yes/Never)\n"))
cat(sprintf("Total Samples: %d\n", nrow(meco_dataset$sample_table)))
cat(sprintf("Taxonomic Levels Analyzed: %s\n", paste(taxonomic_levels, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("Feature Selection: Boruta Algorithm\n"))
cat(sprintf("Cross-Validation: 5-fold CV\n\n"))

cat(sprintf("Total Models Trained: %d\n", length(all_results)))
cat(sprintf("Models per Taxonomic Level: %d (%d with FS, %d without FS)\n", 
            length(methods_to_test) * 2,
            length(methods_to_test), 
            length(methods_to_test)))

cat(sprintf("Successful Models: %d\n", nrow(successful_results)))
cat(sprintf("Failed Models: %d\n", length(all_results) - nrow(successful_results)))

if(nrow(successful_results) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\nBest Overall Model: %s - %s (%s)\n", 
              best_model$Taxonomic_Level, best_model$Method, best_model$Feature_Selection))
  cat(sprintf("Best Model Accuracy: %.1f%%\n", best_model$Accuracy * 100))
  cat(sprintf("Best Model Balanced Accuracy: %.1f%%\n", best_model$Balanced_Accuracy * 100))
  
  # Performance interpretation
  if(best_model$Balanced_Accuracy > 0.75) {
    interpretation <- "Excellent predictive performance - strong microbiome signal for malaria history"
  } else if(best_model$Balanced_Accuracy > 0.65) {
    interpretation <- "Good predictive performance - moderate microbiome signal detected"
  } else if(best_model$Balanced_Accuracy > 0.55) {
    interpretation <- "Moderate predictive performance - weak but detectable microbiome signal"
  } else {
    interpretation <- "Limited predictive performance - microbiome signal for malaria history is weak"
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("\nInterpretation: %s\n", interpretation))
  
  # Taxonomic level analysis
  cat(sprintf("\nTaxonomic Level Performance Summary:\n"))
  for(tax_level in taxonomic_levels) {
    level_results <- successful_results[successful_results$Taxonomic_Level == tax_level, ]
    if(nrow(level_results) > 0) {
      avg_performance <- mean(level_results$Balanced_Accuracy)
      best_performance <- max(level_results$Balanced_Accuracy)
      cat(sprintf("- %s: Avg=%.3f, Best=%.3f (%d successful models)\n", 
                  tax_level, avg_performance, best_performance, nrow(level_results)))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("- %s: No successful models\n", tax_level))
    }
  }
}

cat("\nGenerated Files:\n")
cat("=== FirstYear Folder ===\n")
cat("- FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_results.csv: Detailed performance metrics\n")
cat("- FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_performance.pdf: Performance comparison plot\n")
cat("- FirstYearmicrobiome_performance_heatmap.pdf: Summary heatmap\n")
cat("\n=== Root Folder ===\n")
cat("- FirstYearmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData: Complete R workspace\n")

```


# DAR Consensus Analysis

Verbatim from `DAR_Analysis.R`. Loads from the RData file saved by the
6W classifier chunk above.

```{r dar-analysis, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(123)
library(dar)
library(ALDEx2)
library(DESeq2)
library(metagenomeSeq)
library(Maaslin2)
library(future)
has_lefser <- requireNamespace("lefser", quietly = TRUE)
dir.create("DAR_Genus", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("DAR_Species", showWarnings = FALSE)
if (has_lefser) library(lefser) else cat("Note: lefser not available, step_lefse skipped.\n")
# 1. Load data and prepare
load("SixWeekmicrobiome_multi_taxonomic_analysis.RData")
meco_da <- meco_dataset$clone(deep = TRUE)

## 2A. Recreate Predicted_Group
best_info  <- successful_results[1, ]
method_map <- list("Random Forest" = "rf", "SVM (Radial Kernel)" = "svmRadial",
                   "SVM (Linear Kernel)" = "svmLinear", "Logistic Regression" = "glm",
                   "k-Nearest Neighbors" = "knn", "Naive Bayes" = "nb")
m_code     <- method_map[[as.character(best_info$Method)]]
fs_code    <- ifelse(best_info$Feature_Selection == "With FS","with_fs","without_fs")
best_name  <- paste0(best_info$Taxonomic_Level,"_",m_code,"_",fs_code)
pred       <- predict(all_results[[best_name]]$model$res_train,
                      newdata = all_results[[best_name]]$model$data_feature)
meco_da$sample_table$Predicted_Group <- factor(pred,
                                               levels = c("Never","Yes"),
                                               labels = c("Resistant","Susceptible"))

# 2. Convert to phyloseq
ps <- meco2phyloseq(meco_da)

# 3. Genus-level analysis
rec_genus <- recipe(ps, "Predicted_Group", "Genus") %>%
  step_wilcox(rarefy = FALSE) %>%
  step_aldex(rarefy = FALSE, mc.samples = 128) %>%
  step_deseq(rarefy = FALSE) %>%
  step_corncob(rarefy = FALSE, filter_discriminant = FALSE) %>%
  step_metagenomeseq(rarefy = FALSE, rm_zeros = 0.01) %>%
  step_maaslin(rarefy = FALSE, min_prevalence = 0)
if (has_lefser) rec_genus <- rec_genus %>% step_lefse()

future::plan(multisession, workers = 6)
da_results_genus <- prep(rec_genus, parallel = FALSE)
results_genus <- bake(da_results_genus, count_cutoff = 2)
cool(results_genus)

# 4. Genus visualizations 

# A) Save as Multi-page PDF
pdf(file.path("DAR_Genus", "DAR_Genus_Plots.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
exclusion_plt(da_results_genus)
corr_heatmap(da_results_genus, font_size = 10)
mutual_plt(da_results_genus, top_n = 24)
dev.off()

# B) Save as High-Res PNGs (Separate files)
png(file.path("DAR_Genus", "DAR_Genus_Exclusion.png"), width = 2400, height = 1800, res = 300)
exclusion_plt(da_results_genus)
dev.off()

png(file.path("DAR_Genus", "DAR_Genus_Heatmap.png"), width = 2400, height = 1800, res = 300)
corr_heatmap(da_results_genus, font_size = 10)
dev.off()

png(file.path("DAR_Genus", "DAR_Genus_Mutual.png"), width = 3000, height = 2000, res = 300)
mutual_plt(da_results_genus, top_n = 24)
dev.off()

# Save Main Outputs to DAR_Genus folder
write.csv(cool(results_genus), file.path("DAR_Genus", "dar_consensus_genus_taxa.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
save(da_results_genus, file = file.path("DAR_Genus", "dar_full_results_genus.RData"))
export_steps(rec_genus, file.path("DAR_Genus", "dar_recipe_steps_genus.json"))

# List the DA methods in results_genus
method_names <- names(results_genus@results)

# Prepare a list to store significant results by method
signif_tables_genus <- list()

# Loop over DA methods and extract significant genera
for (method in method_names) {
  df <- results_genus@results[[method]]$Predicted_Group
  if ("signif" %in% names(df)) {
    signif_df <- df[df$signif == TRUE, ]
    if (nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
      signif_tables_genus[[method]] <- signif_df
    }
  } else if ("padj" %in% names(df)) {
    signif_df <- df[df$padj < 0.05, ]
    if (nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
      signif_tables_genus[[method]] <- signif_df
    }
  }
}

# Print out summary and write to DAR_Genus folder
for (method in names(signif_tables_genus)) {
  cat("\n=== Significant Genera for", method, "===\n")
  print(signif_tables_genus[[method]])
  
  # Write file
  outname <- file.path("DAR_Genus", paste0("significant_genera_", method, ".csv"))
  write.csv(signif_tables_genus[[method]], outname, row.names = FALSE)
}


################################################################################
## DAR CONSENSUS ANALYSIS AT SPECIES LEVEL
################################################################################
cat("\n=== START DAR CONSENSUS ANALYSIS: SPECIES LEVEL ===\n")

# Create Output Directory for Species
if (!dir.exists("DAR_Species")) {
  dir.create("DAR_Species")
}

# 5. Species-level analysis (using same ps object)
rec_species <- recipe(ps, "Predicted_Group", "Species") %>%
  step_wilcox(rarefy = FALSE) %>%
  step_aldex(rarefy = FALSE, mc.samples = 128) %>%
  step_deseq(rarefy = FALSE) %>%
  step_corncob(rarefy = FALSE, filter_discriminant = FALSE) %>%
  step_metagenomeseq(rarefy = FALSE, rm_zeros = 0.01) %>%
  step_maaslin(rarefy = FALSE, min_prevalence = 0)
if (has_lefser) rec_species <- rec_species %>% step_lefse()

future::plan(multisession, workers = 6)
da_results_species <- prep(rec_species, parallel = FALSE)
results_species <- bake(da_results_species, count_cutoff = 2)
cool(results_species)

# 6. Species visualizations 

# A) Save as Multi-page PDF
pdf(file.path("DAR_Species", "DAR_Species_Plots.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
exclusion_plt(da_results_species)
corr_heatmap(da_results_species, font_size = 10)
mutual_plt(da_results_species, steps = steps_ids(rec_species, type = "da"), comparisons = NULL, count_cutoff = NULL, top_n = 6)
dev.off()

# B) Save as High-Res PNGs (Separate files)
png(file.path("DAR_Species", "DAR_Species_Exclusion.png"), width = 2400, height = 1800, res = 300)
exclusion_plt(da_results_species)
dev.off()

png(file.path("DAR_Species", "DAR_Species_Heatmap.png"), width = 2400, height = 1800, res = 300)
corr_heatmap(da_results_species, font_size = 10)
dev.off()

png(file.path("DAR_Species", "DAR_Species_Mutual.png"), width = 3000, height = 2000, res = 300)
mutual_plt(da_results_species, steps = steps_ids(rec_species, type = "da"), comparisons = NULL, count_cutoff = NULL, top_n = 6)
dev.off()

# Save Main Outputs to DAR_Species folder
write.csv(cool(results_species), file.path("DAR_Species", "dar_consensus_species_taxa.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
save(da_results_species, file = file.path("DAR_Species", "dar_full_results_species.RData"))
export_steps(rec_species, file.path("DAR_Species", "dar_recipe_steps_species.json"))

cat("\n=== DAR CONSENSUS ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")

# List the names of DA methods
method_names <- names(results_species@results)

# Create an empty list to store significant results by method
signif_tables <- list()

# Loop through each DA method
for (method in method_names) {
  df <- results_species@results[[method]]$Predicted_Group
  # Try "signif" column (dar standard), or "padj" if not present
  if ("signif" %in% names(df)) {
    signif_df <- df[df$signif == TRUE, ]
    if (nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
      signif_tables[[method]] <- signif_df
    }
  } else if ("padj" %in% names(df)) {
    signif_df <- df[df$padj < 0.05, ]
    if (nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
      signif_tables[[method]] <- signif_df
    }
  }
}

# Print a summary and write files to DAR_Species folder
for (method in names(signif_tables)) {
  cat("\n=== Significant Species for", method, "===\n")
  print(signif_tables[[method]])
  
  outname <- file.path("DAR_Species", paste0("significant_species_", method, ".csv"))
  write.csv(signif_tables[[method]], outname, row.names = FALSE)
  
}
```



# Reviewer-Requested Analyses

All new analyses requested by Reviewers 1 and 2. Each section builds its
own fresh data objects from `combinedcongo` to avoid any mutation issues
from upstream classifier analyses.

## Prepare features for caret analyses

```{r reviewer-prep}
set.seed(123)

# Build fresh meco object from original phyloseq (never reuse meco_dataset)
meco_rev <- phyloseq2meco(combinedcongo)
meco_rev$tidy_dataset()
meco_rev$tax_table <- base::subset(meco_rev$tax_table, Kingdom %in% c("k__Archaea", "k__Bacteria"))
meco_rev$filter_pollution(taxa = c("mitochondria", "chloroplast"))
st_rev <- subset(meco_rev$sample_table, Type == "sample")
st_rev$BeforeMalaria <- as.character(st_rev$BeforeMalaria)
st_rev$BeforeMalaria[is.na(st_rev$BeforeMalaria)] <- "Never"
st_rev <- subset(st_rev, BeforeMalaria %in% c("Yes", "Never"))
st_rev <- subset(st_rev, Time == "6W")
st_rev$BeforeMalaria <- factor(st_rev$BeforeMalaria, levels = c("Yes", "Never"))
meco_rev$sample_table <- st_rev
meco_rev$tidy_dataset()

# Use trans_classifier to get the EXACT same feature matrix the classifier uses
cls_rev <- trans_classifier$new(
  dataset = meco_rev, y.response = "BeforeMalaria", x.predictors = "Species"
)
features_full <- as.data.frame(cls_rev$data_feature)
features_full[is.na(features_full)] <- 0

# Outcome vector
outcome <- factor(
  meco_rev$sample_table[rownames(features_full), "BeforeMalaria"],
  levels = c("Yes", "Never")
)
outcome <- relevel(outcome, ref = "Never")

# Covariates
risk_matched <- risk_df[match(
  meco_rev$sample_table[rownames(features_full), "Individual"],
  risk_df$Individual
), ]

st_cov <- meco_rev$sample_table[rownames(features_full), ]
st_cov$bednet_binary <- ifelse(st_cov$X27_Bednet.y %in% c("Siku zote", "Busiku tu"), 1, 0)
st_cov$antibiotic_6w <- as.numeric(as.character(st_cov$Med_Antibiotic))
st_cov$antibiotic_6w[is.na(st_cov$antibiotic_6w)] <- 0
st_cov$infant_sex_male <- ifelse(st_cov$Baby.sex.x == "M", 1, 0)
st_cov$malaria_risk_score <- risk_matched$malaria_risk_score
st_cov$malaria_risk_score[is.na(st_cov$malaria_risk_score)] <- median(risk_matched$malaria_risk_score, na.rm = TRUE)

cat(sprintf("Features: %d samples x %d species\n", nrow(features_full), ncol(features_full)))
cat(sprintf("Outcome levels: %s\n", paste(levels(outcome), collapse = ", ")))
print(table(outcome))
```

## Repeated stratified cross-validation (10 x 5-fold)

```{r reviewer-repeated-cv, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(123)
tc_rep <- caret::trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", number = 5, repeats = 10,
                               classProbs = TRUE, summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary,
                               savePredictions = "final")
model_rep_cv <- caret::train(features_full, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_rep,
                              metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = expand.grid(k = c(3, 5, 7)))
print(model_rep_cv)

cv_res <- model_rep_cv$resample
cat(sprintf("\nRepeated CV (50 folds): AUC = %.3f +/- %.3f\n", mean(cv_res$ROC), sd(cv_res$ROC)))
cat(sprintf("  Sensitivity: %.3f +/- %.3f\n", mean(cv_res$Sens), sd(cv_res$Sens)))
cat(sprintf("  Specificity: %.3f +/- %.3f\n", mean(cv_res$Spec), sd(cv_res$Spec)))
```

```{r reviewer-cv-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Repeated CV performance distribution."}
cv_res %>% dplyr::select(ROC, Sens, Spec) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Metric = dplyr::recode(Metric, ROC = "AUC", Sens = "Sensitivity", Spec = "Specificity")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Metric, y = Value, fill = Metric)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) + geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4393C3", "#D6604D", "#92C5DE")) +
  ylim(0, 1) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Repeated 5-Fold CV (10 Repeats)", y = "Value", x = NULL)
p_cv <- last_plot()
save_fig(p_cv, "FigR_repeated_CV", width = 8, height = 6)
```

## Leave-one-out cross-validation

```{r reviewer-loocv, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(456)
tc_loo <- caret::trainControl(method = "LOOCV", classProbs = TRUE,
                               summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary,
                               savePredictions = "final")
model_loo <- caret::train(features_full, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_loo,
                           metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = expand.grid(k = c(3, 5, 7)))
loocv_preds <- model_loo$pred %>% dplyr::filter(k == model_loo$bestTune$k)
loocv_cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(loocv_preds$pred, loocv_preds$obs, positive = "Never")
print(loocv_cm)
```

## Permutation testing (1,000 permutations)

```{r reviewer-permutation, cache=TRUE}
n_perm <- 1000
best_k <- model_rep_cv$bestTune$k
tc_perm <- caret::trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, classProbs = TRUE,
                                summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary)

set.seed(789)
obs_model <- caret::train(features_full, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_perm,
                           metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = data.frame(k = best_k))
obs_auc <- max(obs_model$results$ROC)

set.seed(101)
null_aucs <- numeric(n_perm)
pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_perm, style = 3)
for (i in seq_len(n_perm)) {
  pm <- tryCatch(caret::train(features_full, sample(outcome), method = "knn",
                               trControl = tc_perm, metric = "ROC",
                               tuneGrid = data.frame(k = best_k)),
                  error = function(e) NULL)
  null_aucs[i] <- if (!is.null(pm)) max(pm$results$ROC) else NA
  setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
}
close(pb)
null_aucs <- null_aucs[!is.na(null_aucs)]
perm_p <- (sum(null_aucs >= obs_auc) + 1) / (length(null_aucs) + 1)

cat(sprintf("\nObserved AUC: %.3f\nNull AUC: %.3f +/- %.3f\nPermutation p = %.4f\n",
            obs_auc, mean(null_aucs), sd(null_aucs), perm_p))
```

```{r reviewer-perm-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Permutation null distribution."}
ggplot(data.frame(AUC = null_aucs), aes(x = AUC)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "grey70", color = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = obs_auc, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = obs_auc + 0.02, y = Inf, vjust = 2, hjust = 0,
           label = sprintf("Observed = %.3f\np = %.4f", obs_auc, perm_p), color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Permutation Test", x = "AUC (5-fold CV)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_perm <- last_plot()
save_fig(p_perm, "FigR_permutation_test", width = 8, height = 6)
```

## Sensitivity analyses (covariates)

```{r reviewer-sensitivity, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(202)
knn_grid <- data.frame(k = best_k)
tc_sens <- caret::trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", number = 5, repeats = 10,
                                classProbs = TRUE, summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary)

feat_A <- features_full
feat_B <- cbind(features_full, bednet = st_cov$bednet_binary)
feat_C <- cbind(features_full, bednet = st_cov$bednet_binary,
                 abx = st_cov$antibiotic_6w, sex = st_cov$infant_sex_male)
feat_D <- cbind(feat_C, risk_score = st_cov$malaria_risk_score)

mods <- list(
  "A: Microbiome only"  = caret::train(feat_A, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_sens, metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = knn_grid),
  "B: + Bednet"         = caret::train(feat_B, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_sens, metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = knn_grid),
  "C: + Bednet/Abx/Sex" = caret::train(feat_C, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_sens, metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = knn_grid),
  "D: + All covariates" = caret::train(feat_D, outcome, method = "knn", trControl = tc_sens, metric = "ROC", tuneGrid = knn_grid)
)
# NOTE: preProcess not applied. All models use unscaled features for
# consistency with the original k-NN classifier. The sensitivity analysis
# tests the *relative* impact of adding covariates to the same model
# framework, not absolute performance.

sens_results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(mods), function(nm) {
  r <- mods[[nm]]$resample
  data.frame(Model = nm, AUC = mean(r$ROC), AUC_SD = sd(r$ROC),
             Sens = mean(r$Sens), Spec = mean(r$Spec))
}))
print(sens_results)

# Paired comparisons
cat("\n=== Paired t-tests vs. Base Model (A) ===\n")
auc_A <- mods[["A: Microbiome only"]]$resample$ROC
for (nm in names(mods)[-1]) {
  tt <- t.test(auc_A, mods[[nm]]$resample$ROC, paired = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("  A vs %s: diff = %+.3f, p = %.4f\n", nm, mean(auc_A - mods[[nm]]$resample$ROC), tt$p.value))
}
```

## Post-hoc power analysis

```{r reviewer-power, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Power curve for two-sample comparison."}
n_r <- sum(outcome == "Never"); n_s <- sum(outcome == "Yes")
det_d <- pwr.t2n.test(n1 = n_r, n2 = n_s, power = 0.80, sig.level = 0.05)$d
cat(sprintf("Min detectable effect at 80%% power: d = %.2f (n = %d vs %d)\n", det_d, n_r, n_s))

d_seq <- seq(0.3, 1.8, 0.1)
pow <- sapply(d_seq, function(d) pwr.t2n.test(n1 = n_r, n2 = n_s, d = d, sig.level = 0.05)$power)
ggplot(data.frame(d = d_seq, power = pow), aes(d, power)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "#2166AC") + geom_point(color = "#2166AC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = det_d, linetype = "dotted", color = "#B2182B") +
  labs(title = "Post-Hoc Power Analysis", x = "Cohen's d", y = "Power") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_power <- last_plot()
save_fig(p_power, "FigR_power_analysis", width = 8, height = 6)
```

## Covariate summary table

```{r reviewer-covariate-table}
data.frame(
  Analysis = c("LDA (LEfSe)", "ANCOM-BC", "ANCOM-BC (bednet-adjusted)", "DAR", "Digital PCR",
               "k-NN Classifier (original)", "k-NN + Covariates (sensitivity)"),
  Infant_Sex = c("No", "Yes", "Yes", "No", "No", "No", "Yes (C,D)"),
  Antibiotics = c("No", "Yes", "Yes", "No", "No", "No", "Yes (C,D)"),
  Bednet_6W = c("No", "No", "Yes", "No", "No", "No", "Yes (B,C,D)"),
  Risk_Score = c("No", "No", "No", "No", "No", "No", "Yes (D)"),
  Breastfeeding = rep("No*", 7),
  check.names = FALSE
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Covariates by analysis. *94-100% exclusively breastfed at 6W.") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```



# Interpretive Results Summary

## Original analyses

The classifier pipeline tested 72 model configurations across six taxonomic levels and six algorithms. The best-performing model was **species-level k-NN with Boruta feature selection**:

- 90.0% balanced accuracy (5-fold CV on 75% training set), 91.7% overall accuracy
- 100% sensitivity, 80.0% specificity, 87.5% precision, 93.3% F1
- **6 features selected by Boruta** from the 144 species-level features

The held-out test set (n = 12) classified 9/12 correctly (75% accuracy, 95% CI: 43--95%, kappa = 0.50, p = 0.19). The wide CI is expected given the small test set.

Top features by composite importance: *S. salivarius* (10.37), *R. mucilaginosa* (9.66), *C. aerofaciens* (8.96), *K. pneumoniae* (8.87), *E. lenta* (6.97) were associated with susceptibility; *C. avidum* (5.29), *B. breve* (4.95), *M. micronuciformis* (4.68) were associated with resistance.

DAR consensus analysis identified 5 genera (*Bifidobacterium*, *Streptococcus*, *Lacticaseibacillus*, *Lactobacillus*, *Megasphaera*) and 6 species as significant by $\geq$2 methods.
